document.title: Medical devices — Guidance on the application of ISO 14971

sourceSummary.oneLine: ISO/TR 24971:2020 provides guidance for applying ISO 14971:2019 risk management to medical devices.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as ISO guidance for developing, implementing and maintaining a medical device risk management process conforming to ISO 14971:2019.
keyPoints: The document provides guidance on the development, implementation and maintenance of a risk management system for medical devices according to ISO 14971:2019.
It applies guidance to active, non-active, implantable, non-implantable, software as medical devices and in vitro diagnostic medical devices.

Manufacturers are expected to establish and maintain risk management files, risk management plans, risk analysis, risk control, residual risk evaluation, management review and production/post-production activities.

The document includes informative annexes on hazards, analysis techniques, risk acceptability policy, information for safety, international standards, security risks, legacy devices and IVD medical devices.

entities.name: ISO
entities.evidence: ISO (the International Organization for Standardization) is a worldwide federation of national standards bodies (ISO member bodies).

entities.name: IEC
entities.evidence: ISO collaborates closely with the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) on all matters of electrotechnical standardization.

entities.name: ISO/TC 210
entities.evidence: This document was prepared jointly by Technical Committee ISO/TC 210, Quality management and corresponding general aspects for medical devices, and Subcommittee IEC/SC 62A

entities.name: IEC/SC 62A
entities.evidence: This document was prepared jointly by Technical Committee ISO/TC 210... and Subcommittee IEC/SC 62A, Common aspects of electrical equipment used in medical practice.

entities.name: ISO 14971:2019
entities.evidence: ISO 14971:2019, Medical devices — Application of risk management to medical devices

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: ISO 14971:2019 requires that the manufacturer establishes, implements, documents and maintains an ongoing risk management process throughout the life cycle of the medical device.

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: The risk management plan describes the scope of the risk management activities, the responsibilities and authorities of those involved, the criteria for risk acceptability, the production and post-production information to be collected and reviewed for the medical device

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: ISO 14971:2019 requires the manufacturer to establish and maintain a risk management file, which contains records and other documents created during risk management activities for the medical device throughout its life cycle

concepts.name: Risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The risk analysis process consists of the following steps... intended use... characteristics... hazards and hazardous situations... estimation of risks for each hazardous situation.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Reasonably foreseeable misuse is defined as use of the medical device in a way not intended by the manufacturer, but which can result from readily predictable human behaviour.

concepts.name: Risk control option analysis
concepts.evidence: Several options exist to reduce risks associated with a medical device. These can be used alone or in combination.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

concepts.name: Overall residual risk
concepts.evidence: ISO 14971:2019 requires that the overall residual risk be evaluated in relation to the benefits of the intended use of the medical device

concepts.name: Production and post-production activities
concepts.pageTarget: Medical Device Production and Post-Production Activities
concepts.evidence: Monitoring of production and post-production information is the critical step that enables medical device manufacturers to close the feedback loop and to make risk management a continuous life cycle process.

concepts.name: Security risks
concepts.evidence: The risk management process described in ISO 14971:2019 can be applied to hazards and risks associated with the security of the medical device.

concepts.name: Information for safety
concepts.evidence: Information for safety is a risk control measure that should be used only after the manufacturer has determined that (further) risk reduction by other measures is not practicable.

concepts.name: IVD medical device risk management
concepts.evidence: The purpose of this annex is to provide guidance for the application of particular aspects of ISO 14971:2019 to in vitro diagnostic medical devices.

evidence.claim: The document is ISO/TR 24971:2020(E), a Technical Report.
evidence.evidenceText: Reference number ISO/TR 24971:2020(E) TECHNICAL REPORT

evidence.claim: The document title is Medical devices — Guidance on the application of ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical devices — Guidance on the application of ISO 14971

evidence.claim: The document is the second edition dated 2020-06.
evidence.evidenceText: Second edition 2020-06

evidence.claim: The second edition cancels and replaces the first edition and was technically revised.
evidence.evidenceText: This second edition cancels and replaces the first edition, which has been technically revised.

evidence.claim: The document was prepared jointly by ISO/TC 210 and IEC/SC 62A.
evidence.evidenceText: This document was prepared jointly by Technical Committee ISO/TC 210... and Subcommittee IEC/SC 62A

evidence.claim: The scope is guidance on development, implementation and maintenance of a medical device risk management system according to ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: This document provides guidance on the development, implementation and maintenance of a risk management system for medical devices according to ISO 14971:2019.

evidence.claim: ISO 14971:2019 is the normative reference for the document.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO 14971:2019, Medical devices — Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The terms and definitions in ISO 14971:2019 apply.
evidence.evidenceText: For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in ISO 14971:2019 apply.

evidence.claim: Top management is responsible for establishing and maintaining an effective risk management process.
evidence.evidenceText: Top management has the responsibility to establish and maintain an effective risk management process.

evidence.claim: A risk management plan describes scope, responsibilities, criteria for risk acceptability, production and post-production information and life cycle activities.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk management plan describes the scope of the risk management activities, the responsibilities and authorities of those involved, the criteria for risk acceptability, the production and post-production information to be collected and reviewed

evidence.claim: A risk management file contains records and documents created during risk management activities throughout the medical device life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO 14971:2019 requires the manufacturer to establish and maintain a risk management file, which contains records and other documents created during risk management activities for the medical device throughout its life cycle

evidence.claim: Risk analysis includes intended use, safety-related characteristics, hazards and hazardous situations, and risk estimation.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk analysis process consists of... description of the intended use... identification of the characteristics... identification of hazards and hazardous situations... estimation of risks

evidence.claim: The standard does not specify levels of acceptable risk.
evidence.evidenceText: The standard, however, does not specify levels of acceptable risk.

evidence.claim: Risk control options include inherently safe design, protective measures and information for safety in descending priority.
evidence.evidenceText: Options a) to c) are listed in descending order of priority with regard to their generally recognised effectiveness in reducing risk.

evidence.claim: Residual risks are evaluated by the same method and criteria as initial risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Residual risks are evaluated by the same method and with the same criteria for risk acceptability as the initial risks.

evidence.claim: Benefit-risk analysis determines whether residual risk is outweighed by expected benefits of intended use.
evidence.evidenceText: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

evidence.claim: The overall residual risk evaluation considers all residual risks together in relation to benefits of intended use.
evidence.evidenceText: This consideration takes into account the contributions of all residual risks together in relation to the benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

evidence.claim: Risk management review is performed after implementation and verification of all risk control measures but before commercial release.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk management review is performed after implementation and verification of all risk control measures but prior to commercial release of the medical device.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production monitoring closes the feedback loop and supports a continuous life cycle risk management process.
evidence.evidenceText: Monitoring of production and post-production information is the critical step that enables medical device manufacturers to close the feedback loop and to make risk management a continuous life cycle process.
evidence.pageTarget: Medical Device Production and Post-Production Activities

evidence.claim: Security in the document includes cybersecurity and data and systems security.
evidence.evidenceText: Security in this document includes cybersecurity and data and systems security.

evidence.claim: International standards can represent generally acknowledged state of the art in risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: Generally, international standards can be considered to represent the generally acknowledged state of the art.
evidence.pageTarget: International Standards in Medical Device Risk Management

evidence.claim: Annex H provides guidance for applying ISO 14971:2019 to IVD medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose of this annex is to provide guidance for the application of particular aspects of ISO 14971:2019 to in vitro diagnostic medical devices.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the provided source text identifies an ISO Technical Report prepared by ISO/TC 210 and IEC/SC 62A; FDA authority is not supported by the source text.
No product models are identified in the source text; productSignal is Unclassified.
